Read-
Financial stability of farmers if farmers' fruit crops are insured against weather hazards
Priority in the state is based on reconstructed climate to help with sustainability.
Fruit insurance scheme is being implemented. Due to various climatic hazards, the productivity of the fruit is being affected.
Adverse effects result in a large-scale reduction in yield. Alternately, farmers do not get the expected yield.
The farmers face a financial crisis on account of all these factors.
Restructured climate under Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana as a measure to get compensation
Based Fruit Insurance Scheme in the state for three years: Year 2021-22, Year 2022-23 and Year 2023-24
Mangoes include oranges, melons, cashews, pomegranates, mangoes, bananas, grapes (A and B), experimental strawberries, and
